title: 49ers rookie could be the surprise of the NFL
description: One San Francisco 49ers rookie has the potential to be big.
fullText:
49ers rookie could be the surprise of the NFL originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Will this be the year the San Francisco 49ers rise up to the top and once again regain the crown? Maybe. It would certainly help to have a star rookie on their roster.

As it turns out, the 49ers do have a budding star in their midst. One NFL analyst sees incredible potential in one 49ers rookie and believes he could upset Fernando Mendoza and become the NFL Offensive Rookie of the Year.

On Monday, John Breech of CBS Sports released a feature about the biggest storylines of the season. He has the 49ers in here for a good reason.

In the piece, Breech says that he believes the 49ers have a gem in wide receiver De'Zhaun Stribling. As for the numbers, last season with the Ole Miss Rebels, Stribling caught 55 passes for 811 yards and had the most receiving touchdowns on the team with six. Breech points out that he shined in the team's College Football Playoff quarterfinal against Georgia, too, catching seven passes for 122 yards.

"Surely he can find a role in this efficient San Francisco 49ers offensive attack -- and should have the opportunity, too," he adds. "Jauan Jennings was the most reliable wide receiver Shanahan had over the past couple of seasons, but he's now gone, and Stribling has the potential to wear the hat he did in this scheme."

If Mendoza doesn't get the playing time that he would need to be named the NFL Offensive Rookie of the Year, there's a strong case to be made that Stribling could sneak up and snag that honor away from him. That would be a good thing for the 49ers.

title: Jason Robertson signs $12 million deal with Dallas Stars to avoid arbitration hearing
description: Jason Robertson and the Dallas Stars will avoid a salary arbitration hearing after the high-scoring forward signed a $12 million contract for next season.  The Stars announced the deal Tuesday night, a day before Robertson's 27th birthday and 2 1/2 weeks after he had filed for arbitration.  “Jason has been a cornerstone of our franchise since he was drafted in 2017,” Stars general manager Jim Nill said.
fullText:
FRISCO, Texas (AP) — Jason Robertson and the Dallas Stars will avoid a salary arbitration hearing after the high-scoring forward signed a $12 million contract for next season.

The Stars announced the deal Tuesday night, a day before Robertson's 27th birthday and 2 1/2 weeks after he had filed for arbitration.

“Jason has been a cornerstone of our franchise since he was drafted in 2017,” Stars general manager Jim Nill said. “A dynamic and skilled player, he has been one of the league’s top offensive threats since making his NHL debut."

Robertson, who has 490 points (213 goals and 277 assists) in his 456 regular-season career games with the Stars, could become an unrestricted free agent for the first time next summer.

His $12 million salary for 2026-27 matches forward Mikko Rantanen for the highest on the Stars. Rantanen just completed the first season of the $96 million, eight-year extension he signed to facilitate the deadline trade that brought him to Dallas from Carolina in March 2025.

Nill had acknowledged at the conclusion of the NHL draft that the Stars had a sign-and-trade deal in place to send Robertson to Seattle for the seventh overall pick. While Robertson couldn’t veto that trade, he wouldn’t agree to a long-term deal with the Kraken — a reported $120 million over eight years ($15 million a season).

Robertson had a team-high 96 points (45 goals, 51 assists) this past season. It was his third 40-goal season, and completed a $31 million, four-year deal he got in 2022.

The Stars drafted Robertson 39th overall in the second round of the 2017 draft. He has played all 328 of their regular-season games the past four seasons, scoring 365 points (155 goals and 210 assists) in that span.

title: Is the Orioles-Red Sox Game Postponed Today? When Will it be Played?
description: Will the Orioles-Red Sox game be delayed today? Latest weather forecast, and what the threat means for Boston's bid to tie franchise record. The post Is the Orioles-Red Sox Game Postponed Today? When Will it be Played? appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Boston Red Sox bring a 14-game winning streak into today’s matchup with the Baltimore Orioles, but unsettled weather could delay first pitch as the club chases a chance to tie the franchise record with a 15th consecutive victory.

If the weather cooperates, Boston will have an opportunity to match a team record that has stood untouched since 1946 â a year that the Red Sox led by Ted Williams and his .346 batting average â got to the World Series. But with a reported 93% chance of precipitation, will the game be played tonight? Or delayed either at first pitch or later during play?

But the weather did not cooperate. At about 5:35 p.m. ET, the Red Sox and MLB made the call to postpone the game the Boston Globe‘s Tim Healey reported. The game was rescheduled as part of a day-night doubleheader on Wednesday, with start times of 1:30 p.m. and 7:10 p.m. ET.

As of 5:20 p.m. ET, just one hour, 50 minutes before game time, no delay or postponement had been announced. But an update could come at any time between that time and first pitch.

aley/status/2079656779617591334?s=20″>the Boston Globe‘s Healey reported that the tarp was coming out to cover the Fenway Park field.

“Noting the off day Thursday for both teams, I strongly advised the Red Sox to postpone today’s game,” the Fenway Weather social media account posted on Tuesday morning.

SP: Eduardo Rivera (LHP)  |  0-0  |  2.89 ERA

Around noon, the Red Sox Triple-A affiliate in Worcester, Massachusetts, about 40 miles west of Boston, announced that its game was postponed, but the major league team had yet to make an announcement.

First pitch at Fenway Park is scheduled for 7:10 p.m. ET. But forecasters are tracking a “heavy” thunderstorm followed by passing showers arriving before or during the game, with southerly wind gusting to 23 mph and up to three-quarters of an inch of rain expected, according to AccuWeather. Fenway sits inside a broader storm corridor stretching from Cleveland through New York and Philadelphia into New England, a system severe enough that the National Weather Service has issued flood watches across multiple Northeast metro areas.

Boston’s ballpark carries a “high” delay-risk designation from DraftKings, alongside Yankee Stadium and Citizens Bank Park, though the outlook notes tonight’s game is more likely to see a late start than an outright postponement. The southerly wind could give fly balls an initial boost before the heavier bands move through and washouts become likely, forecasters noted.

The National Weather Service’s local forecast calls for a high near 83 degrees Tuesday, with rain chances climbing toward a forecast of showers and thunderstorms likely, and precipitation odds reaching 100 percent overnight.

SP: Kyle Bradish (RHP)  |  6-9  |  3.61 ERA

Caleb Durbin’s go-ahead home run over the Green Monster in the eighth inning Monday, aided by four Baltimore errors, snapped the Orioles’ seven-game winning streak and pushed Boston to 51-48. Durbin called it the biggest hit of his career, saying the electricity on the blast is probably top of his personal list.

Interim manager Chad Tracy called Monday’s comeback unbelievable, pointing out his team overcame three separate deficits to escape with the win. Boston’s pitching staff has carried the surge, with the rotation posting a 2.63 ERA during the streak and the bullpen, anchored by closer Aroldis Chapman, checking in even lower at 1.15.

The Red Sox were buried in last place with a 32-46 record in late June before catching fire, going 18-2 over a 20-game stretch built on elite pitching and a suddenly revived offense, according to NBC Sports Boston’s Justin Leger. Willson Contreras, Boston’s home run and RBI leader this season, said tying the record would mean a lot, while cautioning the club can’t get ahead of itself.

Durbin was more direct about the history in front of him. He wants it, he said, adding he can’t speak for the rest of the roster but that he wants to make history, according to Associated Press writer Ken Powtak. Assuming the game is played, Boston will start right-hander Eduardo Rivera tonight, according to Boston Globe reporter Tim Healey. Rivera owns a 2.89 ERA over 9 1/3 innings this season. Baltimore counters with right-hander Kyle Bradish, who has a 6.75 ERA in 10 career starts against the Red Sox.

title: Caitlin Clark hilariously calls out her appearance in Fever video after historic week
description: Caitlin Clark was honored by the Indiana Fever after winning Eastern Conference Player of the Week, but the star guard was more concerned about how she looked in the team’s video.
fullText:
Caitlin Clark hilariously calls out her appearance in Fever video after historic week originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Caitlin Clark spent the past week making WNBA history. Her latest viral moment, however, had nothing to do with one of her deep 3-pointers or pinpoint passes. The Indiana Fever celebrated Clark at practice Tuesday after she was named Eastern Conference Player of the Week for games played from July 13-19. Coach Stephanie White delivered the news in front of the team, prompting cheers and applause from Clark’s teammates.

Clark initially offered a thumbs-up before smiling and joining the celebration. When she later watched the Fever’s video, though, she noticed something else entirely.

“I’m pale but I’m not this pale,” Clark wrote in the comment section of Indiana’s post, according to Athlon Sports.

The self-deprecating response quickly became another reminder that Clark’s sense of humor has remained intact during a spectacular season.

White opened the announcement by telling Indiana’s players, “We got another Player of the Week,” before turning toward Clark. The Fever erupted in celebration as Clark appeared somewhat reluctant to accept the attention. She smiled, clapped and acknowledged the recognition, but her reaction in the comment section ultimately stole the show.

Clark’s remark also gave her teammates more material for an ongoing joke inside the Fever locker room. Lexie Hull and Sophie Cunningham have previously teased Clark about her complexion and suggested she needs to spend more time working on her tan. After Indiana posted the brightly lit practice video, Clark apparently decided to beat her teammates to the punch.

It was a funny conclusion to a week defined by her historic production.

MORE: Caitlin Clark’s decision leaves door open for WNBA rookie to steal spotlight

Clark averaged 25 points, 7.7 assists and 2.3 rebounds over three games during the award period. She shot 43.5 percent from the field while helping Indiana continue its strong season.

The signature performance came Friday against the Seattle Storm. Clark finished with 45 points, 10 assists, four steals, two rebounds and two blocks in the Fever’s 110-107 victory. She became the first player in WNBA history to produce at least 40 points and 10 assists in a single game.

The Indiana guard also became the fastest player in league history to make 200 career 3-pointers. Those achievements made Clark a clear choice for the weekly honor. Her response to receiving it also illustrated why she has connected with so many fans. Even after one of the best performances in WNBA history, she was willing to poke fun at herself over a practice video.

Indiana entered the week with a 16-10 record and another game approaching against the Connecticut Sun. Clark was listed as probable because of a back issue, an encouraging sign for a Fever team attempting to carry its momentum into the All-Star break. If Clark keeps producing at this level, White may have several more awards to announce before the season ends. The Fever might just need to adjust the lighting first.

title: ‘This Is Now My Era’ — Aliff Has Nothing But Respect For Sam-A, But He’s Coming For Interim Gold
description: The 22-year-old star plans to write a new chapter for the strawweight Muay Thai division at The Inner Circle 23.
fullText:
Aliff Sor Dechapan holds nothing but respect for the man who will stand across the ring from him in the main event of The Inner Circle 23. That, however, will not stop him from fulfilling what he believes is his destiny.

The Thai-Malaysian phenom will face multi-time ONE World Champion Sam-A Gaiyanghadao for the ONE Interim Strawweight Muay Thai World Title on the Asia primetime card, which broadcasts live from Bangkok’s revered Lumpinee Stadium on Friday, July 24.

Fans can watch the show exclusively on live.onefc.com.

Every battle in the world’s largest martial arts organization has provided the 22-year-old contender with a lesson, each sharpening a different piece of his game on the road to World Title gold.

Now, standing across from a legendary fighter of Sam-A’s stature, the Thai-Malaysian slugger sees an opportunity that goes beyond simply adding another name to his resume. It is a defining moment he has been building toward for years, and one the Sor Dechapan product intends to seize with both hands.

Aliff, who owns a 62-9 career record, shared:

“Every time I enter the ring, I improve as a fighter. In my last fight against Ramadan [Ondash], my strategy was purely focused on winning because my goal is to fight for the World Title.

“Now, getting to fight Sam-A has made me excited. He is so skilled, which gives me extra motivation and drives me to train even harder. Sam-A is great, but this is now my era.”

Indeed, a win over the living legend would represent an entirely new era – not just for Aliff himself, but for the martial arts community back home in Malaysia, a nation eager to celebrate its first-ever ONE World Champion.

The Kuala Lumpur-based athlete has done everything asked of him to earn this spotlight. He enters the contest riding a five-bout winning streak, looking sharper with each passing appearance since making his promotional debut in April 2023.

Not only does the weight of an entire nation’s hopes rest on his shoulders, but so does the fire that has carried him across 11 tough outings in the promotion:

“I’ve received overwhelming support from Malaysian fans because everyone wants to see a Malaysian become a World Champion. But I don’t feel pressured. Instead, it makes me want to capture the title even more.

“I’m curious to see how much my life will change if I succeed, but before getting there, my sole duty is to perform at my absolute best and bring this belt home.”

That mindset makes one thing abundantly clear: Aliff is expecting nothing less than war against the 378-win veteran, who is two decades his senior.

At 42 years old, Sam-A continues to defy Father Time. The former two-sport, two-division ONE World Champion enjoys a three-bout winning streak of his own leading up to this guaranteed barnburner inside “The Madison Square Garden of the East.”

Underestimating him due to his age would be an easy trap to fall into, but it is one that Aliff has no intention of making.

The Thai-Malaysian rising star told onefc.com:

“A lot of people see this as a matchup between youth and experience, but I will never underestimate him. In the past, many fighters lost simply because they assumed his age would hold him back.

“If he weren’t truly exceptional, he wouldn’t still be competing at this level at age 42.”

That same respect for Sam-A Gaiyanghadao’s greatness, however, does not translate into any lack of belief in his own ability to get the job done.

Aliff has done his homework on the veteran’s world-class game. Having weighed up the 42-year-old’s arsenal against his, the Sor Dechapan affiliate has a clear picture of exactly how he expects the fight to play out once the bell rings at The Inner Circle 23.

“Sam-A’s ultimate weapon is his left kick. His strengths [are also] his ability to adapt and make adjustments inside the ring. As for me, I’ll make full use of my reach advantage. I don’t consider myself at a disadvantage in terms of age, physical condition, or reach.

“I expect him to come forward and press the action, and I’ll be ready to move forward as well. However, I will choose my moments using my own style, combining aggressive attacks with sharp counterstrikes.”

Should everything go according to plan, Aliff believes he could do the unimaginable and put the Evolve MMA representative to sleep en route to capturing the division’s interim crown.

That kind of statement finish would echo the explosive form that first introduced him to fans worldwide, a stretch that saw him rack up three knockouts across four straight matches at the start of his promotional run.

Above all, it would send reigning ONE Strawweight Muay Thai World Champion Prajanchai PongSuphan PK a note on the test awaiting him when he is medically cleared to unify the gold.

“I’m 80 to 90 percent confident that I’ll have the opportunity to finish the fight. Previously, I fought more strategically to secure the win. But for this fight, I’m going to unleash the aggressive style that fans saw during my early days in ONE.”

title: Report: Devon Witherspoon "further along" in contract talks than Christian Gonzalez
description: Patriots cornerback Christian Gonzalez wants a long-term deal, and the Patriots want to sign him to a long-term deal.
fullText:
Patriots cornerback Christian Gonzalez wants a long-term deal, and the Patriots want to sign him to a long-term deal. He shares an agent with Seahawks cornerback Devon Witherspoon, though, and Ian Rapoport reports that Witherspoon is "further along" in talks.

The Seahawks have offered a deal that would pay Witherspoon "upwards of $30 million" annually, per Rapoport. That would make Witherspoon among the highest-paid cornerbacks in the league.

Rams cornerback Trent McDuffie is at the top of the market at the position with an annual average of $31 million. Indianapolis' Sauce Gardner and Houston's Derek Stingley Jr. are at $30 million annually.

Gonzalez, who is scheduled to make $2.259 million this season, won't be far behind Witherspoon in getting a new deal.

The 17th overall pick in 2023 made second-team All-Pro in 2024 and the Pro Bowl in 2025. He has totaled 145 tackles, two interceptions, 24 pass breakups and a sack in three seasons.

title: Patrick Mahomes’ changing appearance through the years has NFL fans saying the same thing
description: It has all the fans talking about the Chiefs signal caller as training camp approaches.
fullText:
Patrick Mahomes’ changing appearance through the years has NFL fans saying the same thing originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Patrick Mahomes has collected three Super Bowl championships, three Super Bowl MVP awards and two NFL MVP honors during his remarkable run with the Kansas City Chiefs. Yet after seeing how the quarterback’s appearance has changed over the years, NFL fans were left wondering whether Mahomes has aged at all.

Mahomes has endured plenty of wear and tear while developing into one of the greatest quarterbacks in NFL history. His latest challenge is a torn ACL suffered in December, an injury that has made his recovery one of the biggest storylines surrounding Kansas City entering the 2026 season.

Despite everything Mahomes has experienced since becoming the Chiefs’ starting quarterback, a collection of photos posted Tuesday had fans making the same observation.

He really does not look much different.

The NFL shared a series of photos on Instagram showing Mahomes from 2019 through the 2026 offseason. Although there were a few noticeable changes, including different headbands, fewer wristbands, shorter hair and a thicker goatee, Mahomes’ overall appearance remained strikingly similar.

That immediately caught the attention of fans.

“He looks the exact same. Is he AI?” one fan wrote, according to Athlon Sports.

“Looks exactly the same! Love you Patrick!” another fan commented.

“He hasn’t aged, he just gets more handsome,” one reaction read.

Another fan asked a simple question: “Does he age??? He looks the same.”

“Never will age!!!!” another person added.

The side-by-side comparison was especially interesting because of everything that has happened during the period covered by the photos.

Mahomes was only beginning to establish himself as the face of the NFL in 2019. He had already won his first league MVP award, but his championship run in Kansas City was just getting started. Since then, Mahomes has led the Chiefs to three Super Bowl victories and established himself as the defining quarterback of his generation. Kansas City selected him with the No. 10 overall pick in the 2017 NFL Draft, and he has spent his entire career with the franchise.

His resume has changed dramatically. His appearance apparently has not.

MORE: It just got a lot harder for Cowboys to trade George Pickens

While fans had fun with the photos, Mahomes is currently dealing with a far more serious issue. The Chiefs quarterback continues to recover from the torn ACL he suffered in December. Reports have suggested that he could return as early as Week 1, although Kansas City will have to carefully monitor his progress leading into the season.

The injury came during a disappointing year for the Chiefs, who missed the playoffs for the first time since Mahomes became their full-time starting quarterback. Kansas City responded by making several notable offseason moves.

The Chiefs re-signed longtime Mahomes target Travis Kelce and acquired reigning Super Bowl MVP Kenneth Walker III to strengthen the offense. They also signed Justin Fields, giving the team an experienced option at quarterback while Mahomes works his way back. Fields could provide important insurance if Mahomes is not cleared for the season opener. If Mahomes is ready, Fields could still give Kansas City additional flexibility as a backup and situational weapon.

The Chiefs are counting on those additions to help them return to the postseason, but their championship hopes will ultimately depend on Mahomes’ health. The quarterback in the 2026 photo might look almost identical to the player pictured seven years earlier. Getting back to playing like that younger version of himself after a major knee injury will be the much more important challenge.

title: Rebecca Lowe details how helicopter ride nearly derailed entire Fox World Cup broadcast
description: Those who tuned in for Fox’s four-hour World Cup final pregame show on Sunday witnessed Rebecca Lowe and the rest of the network’s studio crew travel by land, air, and sea to make it from Brooklyn Bridge Park to MetLife Stadium in time for kickoff. What viewers might not have realized is how the intricate…
fullText:
Those who tuned in for Fox’s four-hour World Cup final pregame show on Sunday witnessed Rebecca Lowe and the rest of the network’s studio crew travel by land, air, and sea to make it from Brooklyn Bridge Park to MetLife Stadium in time for kickoff. What viewers might not have realized is how the intricate plan to transport the crew almost went entirely wrong.

Fox began coverage of the World Cup final with Lowe, Thierry Henry, Zlatan Ibrahimović, and Alexi Lalas live from a set located in Brooklyn Bridge Park, providing a stunning backdrop for the network’s 2026 World Cup swan song. Midway through the show, the crew would leave the set, take a ferry across the East River, drive to a helipad, and chopper to another helipad located near MetLife Stadium, where Spain and Argentina would kick off at 3 p.m. ET.

That itinerary did not go as planned.

Lowe, appearing on the After The Whistle podcast, explained how she and her colleagues got massively delayed, causing them to wonder if they’d even make it to the final at all.

“We had to get from Brooklyn Bridge Park all the way to MetLife while we were on air. So we started with a boat from the ferry stop over the East River to another pier, into a car to take us a couple of piers down to the heliport,” Lowe explained. “Now, I’ve been on a helicopter once, and I swore I’d never go on one again, but then Fox Sports employed me for the World Cup, and I said, ‘Sure, that sounds great.’

“So, we get in the helicopter. It’s supposed to be seven minutes. Wasn’t seven minutes, it was 30 minutes. And let’s just say, Zlatan, God bless him, didn’t say a single word. I was pretty close to that as well, pretty scared, not going to lie. We were circling. I kept seeing the Yankee Stadium again and again and again. …Terrifying, and it’s very bumpy up there, and it was horrible. Alexi Lalas, I’ve never seen him happier. Out the window, pointing out all the amazing sights and sounds, I was like, ‘This guy.’

“We eventually land at the wrong helipad. So now we’re at a random- by the way, we had to be back on air at 1:15. We eventually get out, we film our little link, and eventually realize we’re not at Teterboro, I think is it called? We’re in Kearny, New Jersey. We didn’t know where we were. We were supposed to meet a police escort, a couple of cars, to take us then to MetLife. Well, they were at Teterboro. So now we’re calling them, they come to get us, that’s 15 minutes. We’re filming another piece just saying, ‘We don’t know where we are.’

“And at this point, I’m thinking, ‘Are we going to make it?’ And at this point, we’re about an hour and a half before kickoff of a World Cup final, and I’m thinking, ‘I’ve done this World Cup for 39 days, and I’m not going to get to do the final because I’m going to be in a car on the New Jersey Turnpike.’

“So at this point … we have to be on air for 1:54. So we get into the police escort, we were going 105 miles an hour. 105. And it was very scary. So that was the helicopter — scary. Then we had the police escort — scary. Then we finally get there, we circle the stadium a couple times because no one knows how to get into this place. Pull up, we go under, we get on the elevator, it’s now 1:51. I go on the set at 1:52. We go on air at 1:54.”

It seems like Murphy’s Law hit the Fox Sports World Cup studio at the worst possible time. Luckily, the team was able to make it to the stadium about an hour before the match started, and the only collateral damage from the entire affair was missing an interview with Timothée Chalamet. Oh, well. That’ll just have to wait until 2030.

title: Every PDC 9-darter Luke Littler has hit in his career
description: Luke Littler seems to add to this total every few months.
fullText:
Every PDC 9-darter Luke Littler has hit in his career originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Luke Littler has only been playing PDC darts for a few years now, but he's made an impact on the sport already.

He seemingly produces a 9-darter or at least a chance at hitting one every time he plays in a match.

Let's take a look at every PDC 9-darter Luke Littler has hit in his career.

Littler hit a stunning nine-darter in the first leg of his match against Nathan Aspinall at the 2026 World Matchplay, marking the second time in his career he hit a 9-darter against Aspinall in the first leg of a match.

Littler nearly hit a second one a few legs later, but wired the double on the 9th dart.

Littler was on the right side of history, hitting a 9-darter in one of the best legs we've ever seen take place on a PDC stage.

Littler was fortunate enough to be throwing first, as his opponent Josh Rock left 141 after hitting a pair of 180s himself, but was denied the chance to hit a nine-darter after Littler hit the perfect leg first.

This was the first time Littler struck a PDC nine-darter on D15, which we've seen him continue to attack when he leaves 141 and 147.

Littler has a couple of non-stage nine-darters, including this one that he hit in a floor event in 2025.

Littler's most famous 9-darter came when he was in the biggest match of his life at the time (outside of the 2024 WC final), and out of the second TV break, he found perfection for the first time in his career at a major TV event.

Littler's first Euro Tour 9-darter was hit against Rob Cross, which sent the fans in Belgium into a frenzy.

En route to his first-ever ranked event title, Littler found perfection against Michele Turetta.

Littler's first on-stage PDC 9-darter came on the World Series circuit, just a few weeks after his legendary debut run at the WC.

Littler registered his first career PDC 9-darter in a DT event before he eventually earned his tour card.

title: Andrew Young reveals secret behind Muhammad Ali’s iconic Olympic moment in Atlanta
description: As Atlanta marks the 30th anniversary of the 1996 Summer Olympics, former Atlanta Mayor and U.S. Ambassador Andrew Young is sharing new details about one of the most memorable moments in Olympic history.
fullText:
As Atlanta marks the 30th anniversary of the 1996 Summer Olympics, former Atlanta Mayor and U.S. Ambassador Andrew Young is sharing new details about one of the most memorable moments in Olympic history.

[DOWNLOAD: Free WSB-TV News app for alerts as news breaks]

In an interview with Channel 2’s Karyn Greer, Young reflected on the surprise appearance by boxing legend Muhammad Ali during the Opening Ceremony.

When asked what stood out as one of the best moments of the Games, Young pointed to Ali lighting the Olympic cauldron.

“I think Muhammad Ali lighting the torch. That came from NBC. And they saw Muhammad Ali as a giant, world-class figure that would focus the entire world in one moment on Atlanta, and that’s sort of what happened,” Young said.

According to Young, Ali arrived in Atlanta the day before the Opening Ceremony. Organizers worked to keep his presence under wraps, housing the three-time heavyweight champion in a hotel arranged by the Atlanta Committee for the Olympic Games.

However, keeping Ali out of sight proved difficult.

“It was supposed to be a secret that he was in town,” Young said.

Young recalled that Ali quickly grew restless and wanted to get out and experience the city.

“He didn’t want to be hiding in a hotel room,” Young said. “He called me and said he wanted to get a haircut.”

Young offered to bring a barber to the hotel, but Ali had other plans.

“No, I don’t want to bring no barber up here. I want to go where you go, get your hair cut,” Young recalled Ali saying.

Before Young could arrive, Ali had already made his way to a local barbershop.

“He got somebody to bring him down, but he went down the aisle and in the beauty parlor, and he hugged the ladies, and he shook hands with everybody, and he put on his little rope-a-dope show. And he had a ball,” Young said.

After getting a haircut, Ali visited a seafood restaurant in southwest Atlanta.

“And he bought everybody lunch,” Young said.

As word spread and crowds began to grow, Young worried the secret surrounding the Opening Ceremony could leak to the media.

Despite Ali’s public appearances, organizers managed to keep his role in the ceremony hidden until the moment he stepped into the spotlight and lit the Olympic torch.

“And nobody ever got word of that, but he wanted to be out with the people. He brought a spirit to the games,” Young said. “That may be my biggest thrill.”

title: Jets' Geno Smith gets stern warning ahead of 2026 season
description: Geno Smith and the New York Jets have a quarterback situation.
fullText:
Jets' Geno Smith gets stern warning ahead of 2026 season originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The New York Jets are in a bit of a pickle after their 2025 season, which saw the team with a 3-14 record. But, that doesn't mean all hope is lost. Head coach Aaron Glenn is working hard to get this team in better shape this September than it was last. Will it work? Time will tell.

A lot of the Jets' destiny is in their new quarterback, Geno Smith. Even though Smith hasn't been with the Jets since 2016, he still has a feel for how things in New York work, which should help.

But, ahead of the new season, Smith and the Jets are getting a warning of sorts. In a feature out on Monday, John Breech of CBS Sports names the Jets one of the worst quarterback situations in the NFL right now.

The Jets' quarterback room, including Smith, Cade Klubnik, Bailey Zappe and Brady Cook, is being called one of the worst by Breech. He points out the negatives of the situation.

In the piece, he says that, "Smith went from one of the most underrated quarterbacks in the NFL to one of the toughest to watch last season. The Jets also have fourth-round pick Cade Klubnik from Clemson, Brady Cook, who went 0-4 as a starter last season with two touchdowns and seven interceptions, and Western Kentucky legend Bailey Zappe."

So, it's not just Smith in question. The other quarterbacks are also under the microscope. Either way you slice it, the pressure is on Smith and everyone else in the Jets' camp to turn things around. That's the goal for Glenn this season, as lofty as it seems. Frankly, though, Smith and Glenn are both extremely talent in what they do, so don't count the Jets out.

title: Hardware Mechanisms to Dynamically Throttle AI Performance
description:
As more capable AI models are increasingly integrated into critical computer systems, the lack of control over AI intent motivates safety mechanisms. Existing software safeguards impose only behavioral constraints that can potentially be bypassed by sufficiently intelligent models. While hardware-level safety enforcement has been recognized as an essential last line of defense, few mechanisms have been proposed beyond policy regulations on unauthorized accesses or coarse full-chip shutdown. What is missing is a fine-grained, dynamic intervention mechanism at the architecture level.
  In this paper, we introduce a set of microarchitecture knobs which dynamically control the available hardware resources to limit AI performance at runtime.

title: SEAM-V: A Hybrid-Decoupled RISC-V Vector Processor
description: Data-parallel workloads in deep learning and scientific computing continue to drive demand for higher processor throughput, energy efficiency, and scalability. The RISC-V Vector Extension (RVV) supports scalable execution through a vector-length-agnostic programming model. However, many tightly coupled implementations still rely on the scalar core to supply vector instructions one at a time, making execution susceptible to vector-instruction supply gaps, scalar-side progression delays, and conservative dependence handling in short-vector, loop-tail, and control/memory-interleaved phases. This paper presents SEAM-V, a hybrid-decoupled vector execution architecture for RVV. SEAM-V forms a continuous stream of execute packets (EPs) through task-level decoupling, local instruction supply, and VLIW-style packing.
